prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model, a common method for forecasting stock prices. This model utilizes historical data to estimate two key parameters: annualized drift (expected return) and annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns). For KO, based on historical performance, an estimated annualized drift of approximately 5.04% and an annualized volatility of 16% were used. The prediction horizon is 20 trading days. The point estimate represents the median expected price according to the model, while the confidence intervals define the ranges within which the price is expected to fall with the specified probability, taking into account the inherent uncertainty and volatility of the stock.
